@user1:
Let's make it a `TextField`. Somebody might want to put three 60-character-long columns here, and boom, we're out of space.
results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_team_live_events_columns_uses_textfield_not_charfield__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/posthog/models/team/team.py').read()
    match = re.search('^\\s*live_events_columns\\s*:\\s*ArrayField\\s*=\\s*(.+)$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    assert match is not None, 'Expected Team model to define `live_events_columns`, but it was not found.'

def test_team_live_events_columns_uses_textfield_not_charfield__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/posthog/models/team/team.py').read()
    match = re.search('^\\s*live_events_columns\\s*:\\s*ArrayField\\s*=\\s*(.+)$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    definition = match.group(1)
    assert 'ArrayField(' in definition, 'Expected `live_events_columns` to be an ArrayField definition.'

def test_team_live_events_columns_uses_textfield_not_charfield__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/posthog/models/team/team.py').read()
    match = re.search('^\\s*live_events_columns\\s*:\\s*ArrayField\\s*=\\s*(.+)$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    definition = match.group(1)
    assert 'models.TextField()' in definition, 'Expected `live_events_columns` to use `ArrayField(models.TextField(), ...)` to avoid max_length limits, but it does not. (It may still be using `models.CharField(max_length=...)`.)'

def test_team_live_events_columns_uses_textfield_not_charfield__a4():
    source = open('/workspace/posthog/models/team/team.py').read()
    match = re.search('^\\s*live_events_columns\\s*:\\s*ArrayField\\s*=\\s*(.+)$', source, flags=re.MULTILINE)
    definition = match.group(1)
    assert 'models.CharField' not in definition, 'Expected `live_events_columns` not to use `models.CharField(...)`; it should use `models.TextField()` instead.'

results.test_code:
# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import re

def test_selected_column_display_item_uses_primary_bg_hover_variable__a1():
    source = open('/workspace/frontend/src/lib/components/ResizableTable/TableConfig.scss').read()
    assert 'background-color: var(--primary-bg-hover);' in source, "Expected '.column-display-item.selected' to use 'background-color: var(--primary-bg-hover);' (not --bg-light-blue)."

def test_selected_column_display_item_uses_primary_bg_hover_variable__a2():
    source = open('/workspace/frontend/src/lib/components/ResizableTable/TableConfig.scss').read()
    selected_blocks = re.findall('&\\.selected\\s*\\{[^}]*\\}', source, flags=re.DOTALL)
    assert selected_blocks, "Expected to find at least one '&.selected { ... }' block in TableConfig.scss."

def test_selected_column_display_item_uses_primary_bg_hover_variable__a3():
    source = open('/workspace/frontend/src/lib/components/ResizableTable/TableConfig.scss').read()
    selected_blocks = re.findall('&\\.selected\\s*\\{[^}]*\\}', source, flags=re.DOTALL)
    assert all(('var(--bg-light-blue)' not in block for block in selected_blocks)), "Did not expect 'var(--bg-light-blue)' to be used inside any '&.selected { ... }' block; should use '--primary-bg-hover'."
